display.purpose: Winning-hand verdict only. Appears once, only when isWinningHand returns true.

headline.purpose: Engine suggestion text in the sticky bar.

title.purpose: Region labels and panel headings (Your hand, Ranked targets).

body.purpose: Target descriptions and general prose. Rare in this UI.

label.purpose: Uppercase structural captions (RULESET, NEEDS, WORKING TOWARD). Letter-spacing 0.1em carries the cue.

numeric.purpose: Scores, counts, rank digits. Always uses tabular-nums.

cjk.purpose: Tile suit-marks (萬 / 索 / 筒) and dragon characters (中 / 發). Scoped to .cjk class only.

mono.purpose: Joker JKR stamp and the position JSON textarea.

shadows.name: suggested-tile-glow
shadows.purpose: Engine's suggested discard tile only. The single 'shadow' in the system; everything else is flat.

shadows.name: focused-slot-ring
shadows.purpose: Inner ring on the currently-focused TileSlot, paired with solid border swap.

shadows.name: needed-tile-ring
shadows.purpose: Marginalia Green ring on tiles the top target still needs.

shadows.name: chip-selected-lift
shadows.purpose: Barely-there lift under a selected chip in the segmented control. Almost invisible — the tonal swap is the real signal.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Default easing for tile state changes, hover, and slot focus.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Slightly more emphatic ease. Reserved for engine-suggestion entrances.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Most emphatic ease. Reserved for win-state transitions.

motion.name: duration-deliberate
motion.purpose: Score-bar width changes; the engine 're-thinking' after a pin or edit.

components.name: Tile (number)
components.description: The signature component. A flat 2D mahjong tile face — large numeral + CJK suit-mark below. Information, not button.
components.html: 5萬
components.css: .ds-tile { display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; width: 42px; height: 56px; background: oklch(0.995 0.004 80); border: 1px solid oklch(0.88 0.008 80); border-radius: 6px; user-select: none; padding: 0; transition: box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); font-family: 'Inter Tight', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-tile-rank { font-size: 22px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: -0.02em; margin-top: 5px; font-feature-settings: 'tnum', 'ss01'; } .ds-tile-suit { font-family: 'PingFang SC', 'Hiragino Sans GB', 'Source Han Sans SC', 'Noto Sans CJK SC', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1; opacity: 0.85; margin-bottom: 5px; } .ds-suit-crack .ds-tile-rank, .ds-suit-crack .ds-tile-suit { color: oklch(0.50 0.16 25); } .ds-suit-bamboo .ds-tile-rank, .ds-suit-bamboo .ds-tile-suit { color: oklch(0.46 0.13 145); } .ds-suit-dot .ds-tile-rank, .ds-suit-dot .ds-tile-suit { color: oklch(0.38 0.09 245); }

components.name: Tile (suggested discard)
components.description: A tile being pointed at by the engine. Same face as a regular tile — the ring and lift do all the signalling.
components.html: 7萬
components.css: .ds-tile-suggested { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.46 0.11 252), 0 8px 18px -10px oklch(0.46 0.11 252 / 0.55); transform: translateY(-3px); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-tile-suggested { transform: none; } }

components.name: TileSlot (focused)
components.description: Dashed placeholder where a tile could go. Becomes solid + tinted when focused — the visual target for palette clicks.
components.html: <button type="button" class="ds-slot ds-slot-focused" aria-label="add tile"><svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" aria-hidden="true" width="16" height="16"><path d="M8 3 L8 13 M3 8 L13 8" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.4" stroke-linecap="round" /></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-slot { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 42px; height: 56px; background: transparent; border: 1px dashed oklch(0.78 0.010 80); border-radius: 6px; color: oklch(0.62 0.008 80); cursor: pointer; padding: 0; transition: border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), background-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-slot:hover { border-color: oklch(0.46 0.11 252); color: oklch(0.46 0.11 252); background: oklch(0.93 0.04 252); } .ds-slot-focused { border-style: solid; border-color: oklch(0.46 0.11 252); color: oklch(0.46 0.11 252); background: oklch(0.93 0.04 252); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.46 0.11 252 / 0.18); } .ds-slot svg { width: 40%; height: 40%; opacity: 0.7; }

components.name: Chip (segmented control)
components.description: Segmented selector for ruleset and phase. The selected chip pops via tonal swap, not color.
components.css: .ds-chip-row { display: inline-flex; gap: 2px; padding: 2px; background: oklch(0.965 0.006 80); border-radius: 999px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.88 0.008 80); font-family: 'Inter Tight', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-chip { font-size: 0.8125rem; padding: 0.25rem 0.75rem; border: 0; border-radius: 999px; color: oklch(0.44 0.010 80); font-weight: 500; background: transparent; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-chip:hover { color: oklch(0.20 0.012 80); } .ds-chip:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.46 0.11 252); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-chip-on { background: oklch(0.995 0.004 80); color: oklch(0.20 0.012 80); box-shadow: 0 1px 2px oklch(0 0 0 / 0.04); }

components.name: Panel
components.description: The default surface for grouping content. Hairline-bordered, paper-toned. Never nested.
components.html:
Ranked targets

Panel content lives here.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: oklch(0.995 0.004 80); border: 1px solid oklch(0.88 0.008 80); border-radius: 10px; padding: 1rem; font-family: 'Inter Tight', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; color: oklch(0.20 0.012 80); } .ds-panel-header { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; } .ds-panel-header h2 { margin: 0; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.01em; } .ds-region-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: oklch(0.62 0.008 80); display: inline-block; }

components.name: Ranked Target Row
components.description: Signature reasoning row: rank, description, hairline completion bar, score, needed-tile glyphs. Row, not card.
components.css: .ds-target-row { position: relative; padding: 0.75rem 0; border-bottom: 1px solid oklch(0.88 0.008 80); list-style: none; font-family: 'Inter Tight', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-target-row::before { content: ''; position: absolute; left: -8px; top: 0.75rem; width: 3px; height: 12px; border-radius: 1.5px; background: transparent; transition: background-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-target-top::before { background: oklch(0.93 0.04 252); } .ds-target-pinned::before { background: oklch(0.46 0.11 252); } .ds-target-button { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1.6rem 1fr auto; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; width: 100%; text-align: left; padding: 0; background: none; border: 0; cursor: pointer; color: inherit; } .ds-target-rank { font-size: 0.8125rem; color: oklch(0.62 0.008 80); font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-target-top .ds-target-rank { color: oklch(0.32 0.10 252); } .ds-target-meta { display: grid; gap: 2px; min-width: 0; } .ds-target-pattern { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.20 0.012 80); letter-spacing: -0.005em; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; } .ds-target-rationale { font-size: 0.6875rem; color: oklch(0.44 0.010 80); font-style: italic; } .ds-target-score { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 60px auto; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; } .ds-target-bar { position: relative; display: block; height: 1px; background: oklch(0.88 0.008 80); overflow: hidden; } .ds-target-bar::after { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 0; top: -1px; height: 3px; width: var(--fill, 0%); background: oklch(0.20 0.012 80); border-radius: 1.5px; transition: width 300ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-target-top .ds-target-bar::after { background: oklch(0.46 0.11 252); } .ds-target-num { font-size: 0.8125rem; color: oklch(0.44 0.010 80); font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-target-top .ds-target-num { color: oklch(0.32 0.10 252); }

components.name: Button (primary)
components.description: The single bold action: 'apply' in the JSON editor. Bookmark Indigo only appears here outside engine signals.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-size: 0.8125rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: 0; border-radius: 999px; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.985 0.005 80); background: oklch(0.46 0.11 252); cursor: pointer; font-family: 'Inter Tight', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; transition: background-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.32 0.10 252); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.46 0.11 252); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Link (marginalia action)
components.description: Utility actions like 'load sample / reset / position JSON'. Reads as marginalia, not as a button.
components.css: .ds-link { font-size: 0.6875rem; color: oklch(0.44 0.010 80); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.1em; font-weight: 600; padding: 2px 0; background: none; border: 0; cursor: pointer; font-family: 'Inter Tight', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; transition: color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-link:hover { color: oklch(0.46 0.11 252); }

narrative.overview:
This is the visual language of a study tool, not an app. Imagine a quiet table with a single lamp: the NMJL 2026 card spread open, a notebook beside it, and the player working through a position they want to understand. The page is the table. Mahjong tiles are the only loud elements; everything else is the table around them — paper-tinted surfaces, hairline rules, restrained type. The accent color is a single bookmark slipped between pages, used sparingly and on purpose: where the engine wants to point, not for decoration.

The system rejects three families explicitly. It rejects casino chrome (gambling-table green, gold trim, Vegas typography) — this is study, not play. It rejects toy-mahjong solitaire aesthetics (bright primaries, cartoon faces, confetti) — the user already knows the game; we don't talk down to them. And it rejects the generic SaaS dashboard (metric cards with up-arrows, gradient KPIs, hero-stat grids) — there are no KPIs here, only positions to read. When in doubt, the question is 'what would a paper card-and-notebook setup do?' The answer is usually less.
keyCharacteristics: Paper-tinted neutrals: every surface tinted toward hue 80 at chroma 0.004–0.010.
Hairlines do structural work; thicker means state, not decoration.
Tile glyphs as typography, rendered with canonical CJK suit characters (萬 / 索 / 筒).
The accent is a bookmark — Bookmark Indigo on ≤10% of any screen.
Asymmetric rhythm — the hand row breathes; peripheral regions cluster tighter.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Bookmark Indigo is signal, never decoration. It appears in exactly four places: the engine's suggested-discard ring, the pinned target's left-edge segment, keyboard focus outlines, and the primary button. If you find yourself reaching for it elsewhere, you're doing decoration — stop and use neutral.

rules.name: The Tile-Domain Rule
rules.body: Suit colors (Crack Red, Bamboo Green, Dot Indigo) and dragon colors live only inside tile glyphs. They never appear in chrome — no green panels, no red labels, no indigo backgrounds.

rules.name: The Tinted-Warm Rule
rules.body: Every neutral is tinted toward hue 80 at chroma 0.004–0.010. Never #fff, never #000, never an untinted gray.

rules.name: The Italic-Is-Whisper Rule
rules.body: Italics are used only for the soft voice of the page: hints, empty-state messages, the engine's prose rationale. Never use italics for emphasis — emphasis goes to weight contrast.

rules.name: The Numerals-Align Rule
rules.body: Anywhere numerals stack (scores, counts, ranks), use tabular-nums. The columns are doing work; misaligned digits look like rounding errors.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. No drop shadows, no inset shadows, no gradient overlays. If you reach for a shadow, the answer is almost always a tone change or a hairline.

rules.name: The Ring-Not-Lift Rule
rules.body: State (selected, focused, suggested) renders as a ring outside the element. The element's own face never changes. This keeps tiles especially readable: a suggested tile is still the same tile, just pointed at.

dos: Do keep every neutral tinted toward hue 80 at chroma 0.004–0.010. The paper feel depends on consistency.
Do use Bookmark Indigo only as engine signal: suggested discard, pinned target, focus ring, primary button. ≤10% of any screen.
Do render tiles as flat 2D faces with CJK suit-marks (萬 / 索 / 筒). The canonical glyphs are the cultural artifact; that's why they're loud.
Do let hairlines (1px Ruled Line) do structural work. Thicker means state, never decoration.
Do render state (selected, focused, suggested) as a ring outside the element. The element's face never changes.
Do layer with tone (Aged Vellum / Paper White / Bleached Paper) before reaching for any shadow.
Do use tabular-nums anywhere numerals stack. Scores, counts, ranks must align column-wise.
Do reserve italics for the soft voice — hints, empty states, the engine's prose rationale. Never italics for emphasis.
donts: Don't use #fff or #000. Every neutral is tinted-warm OKLCH.
Don't use suit colors (Crack Red, Bamboo Green, Dot Indigo) on chrome. They live inside tile glyphs only.
Don't add drop shadows for ambient elevation. Use tone change or hairline. The only 'shadow' in the system is the suggested-tile glow.
Don't wrap regions in cards. Most things don't need a container; a region label and some space is usually enough.
Don't nest panels. A panel inside a panel is always wrong.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent. Pinned targets get a 3px corner segment, not a side stripe.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient background). Emphasis through weight or size, never gradients.
Don't reach for SaaS-dashboard metric cards (big number + label + up-arrow delta). Completion scores are hairline bars, not KPI tiles.
Don't style this like a casino app: no felt-green tables, no gold trim, no Vegas typography. This is a Reading Room, not a gambling table.
Don't style this like a toy mahjong solitaire app: no bright primaries, no cartoon tile faces, no confetti on win. The user is studying, not playing.
Don't style this like Stockfish chess analyzers: the framing is inspirational, the aesthetic isn't. No arrow overlays, no board-geometry visualizations.
Don't decorate with Asian-pastiche flourishes (bamboo backgrounds, dragon frames, calligraphic display fonts). The tiles already are the cultural artifact; surrounding them in cosplay cheapens them.
